text:
Hey, we’ve already got some points on the board!

Why not? It’s a free newsletter!

I didn’t really know what to expect with this project. Would it take a while for a Big Ten athlete to snag some points? Or a medal? What were the odds that we’d already see a gold on Day 1? I was a bit worried that it might take some time, but damn! I was wrong.

We have points! Link here for the preview article and scoring system. Sarah Bacon of Minnesota led off with the first medal for the US and the first medal for the Big Ten (and 15 pts in our game) after a fantastic competition in synchronized diving.

But more importantly, CooknBacon!

In the pool, Hunter Armstrong of Ohio State was part of the dominant men’s 4x100m freestyle relay gold medals, netting him 20 points.

Maggie MacNeil of Michigan was part of the fourth-place finishing Canadian women’s 4x100 free relay, and Rafael Miroslaw finished in 7th as part of the German men’s 4x100 free. Finally, Fares Arfa of Ohio State made it to the top 8 in men’s sabre event.

Plenty on tap today, including some more swimming and a bronze medal match for Ohio State’s Eleanor Harvey in fencing. We’ll keep this going for as long as I can keep the data on track! If I’ve missed any Big Ten athletes, let me know in the comments or email me at alma.otter91@gmail.com.

Ohio State: 21

Minnesota: 15

Michigan: 7

Indiana: 2
